message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Obesity is a major risk factor for SARS-CoV-2 infection and COVID-19 severity. The underlying basis of this association is likely complex in nature. The host-cell receptor angiotensin converting enzyme 2 (ACE2) and the type II transmembrane serine protease (TMPRSS2) are important for viral cell entry. It is unclear whether obesity alters expression of
                  <jats:italic>Ace2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>Tmprss2</jats:italic>
                  in the lower respiratory tract. Here, we show that: 1)
                  <jats:italic>Ace2</jats:italic>
                  expression is elevated in the lung and trachea of diet-induced obese male mice and reduced in the esophagus of obese female mice relative to lean controls; 2)
                  <jats:italic>Tmprss2</jats:italic>
                  expression is increased in the trachea of obese male mice but reduced in the lung and elevated in the trachea of obese female mice relative to lean controls; 3) in chow-fed lean mice, females have higher expression of
                  <jats:italic>Ace2</jats:italic>
                  in the lung and esophagus as well as higher
                  <jats:italic>Tmprss2</jats:italic>
                  expression in the lung but lower expression in the trachea compared to males; and 4) in diet-induced obese mice, males have higher expression of
                  <jats:italic>Ace2</jats:italic>
                  in the trachea and higher expression of
                  <jats:italic>Tmprss2</jats:italic>
                  in the lung compared to females, whereas females have higher expression of
                  <jats:italic>Tmprss2</jats:italic>
                  in the trachea relative to males. Our data indicate diet- and sex-dependent modulation of
                  <jats:italic>Ace2</jats:italic>
                  and
                  <jats:italic>Tmprss2</jats:italic>
                  expression in the lower respiratory tract and esophagus. Given the high prevalence of obesity worldwide and a sex-biased mortality rate, we discuss the implications and relevance of our results for COVID-19.
                </jats:p>
title: Obesity alters Ace2 and Tmprss2 expression in lung, trachea, and esophagus in a sex-dependent manner: Implications for COVID-19

reference.article-title: Obesity is Associated with Severe Forms of COVID-19
